title: $applname
text: $applname Return the name of the Application Shell. $applname Return Values String that contains the name of the current application shell (in uppercase). Name of its application server, if component is remote (that is, a service, session service, entity service or report running on a server) . Use Use only in Application Shell and Form components. Description Note: The name is returned with uppercase letters, so this name is not necessarily the same as the application name seen by the operating system. For example, applications built on a Unix platform are created in lowercase, but $applname returns the Uniface application name, which is in uppercase. One use for $applname could be to restrict the use of a global ProcScript to certain (named) applications; a test on the value of $applname at the start of the ProcScript module could prevent use of the module by returning immediately. If you distribute only the object code for your global ProcScript (in UAR files containing /prc/*.prc , for example) and not the source, you can use this technique to prevent unauthorized use of the global ProcScript. Example: Using $applname The following example outputs an initial message and a final message when the Application Shell BOOK is run: ; trigger: apStart putmess "Started application %%$applname at %%$clock" run "MAIN_MENU" putmess "Terminated application %%$applname at %%$clock" Copy Related Links $entname Return the name of the current entity or check for the existence of an entity in the component. $fieldname Return the name of the current field or check for the presence of a specified field in the component. $instancename Return the name of the current component instance.
